theme: An Anecdote from the Antiques Market
text:
Perhaps no place reveals a person's character like a Sunday market, where the surroundings shift from stall to stall and a single anecdote can sum up a whole life. Last sunny day, in bright sunshine, I joined the throng on the left side of the square, content to wander with no fixed agenda. A junior trader, lazy in manner yet ingenious in talk, was trying to advertise an assorted box of magnetic toys and a compact chess set he hoped would render him rich.

Nearby, a patriot in faded jeans had laid out an ornament shaped like a planet, a postcard collection, and the trunk of an old automobile radio. He spoke with such fortitude about the revival of local craft that a small crowd began to conceive real interest. "In actuality," he said, scraping mud from a coin, "these things carry more security than any star on a screen. Heed the past, and it will not annoy you."

The scene felt almost classical. A female violinist played while a duke's portrait, its frame topmost on the wall, looked down with an air both urgent and serene. Someone had begun to embroider a flag; another chewed slowly on a strip of dried meat, perhaps an eggplant relish on the side. The whole panorama was a study in human variety, comprehensive and warm, though now and then an unwelcome jerk of the elbow reminded you to mind your seat at the crowded counter.

Meanwhile, a notary in a grey coat moved through the rows to nullify a forged document and dismiss a dubious claim. He explained, with a perspective sharpened by years of dealing, that to deny fraud is not enough; one must directly oppose it. "It would be thoughtless to incur a debt you cannot repay," he told a nervous young person, "and reckless to ignore the peril of haphazard spending. Such a habit can degrade a family as surely as slavery once crushed the free."

I bought a small print, a finding I had not anticipated, and a scarred wooden box whose lid no longer closed. The seller, prestigious among collectors, was inclined to donate a second piece, an antique mixer for the kitchen, just to oblige me. As the day cooled, the climactic moment came when an old man, his face marked by a scar, began to play a haunting tune. The preferential treatment of beauty over price, the uniformly gentle mood, the amusement of bargaining: all of it made the market a place I would dispatch my friends to without hesitation, certain they too would leave content. Before the excursion ended, a guard checked our identification at the peripheral gate, his manner the hallmark of an invincible discipline. The conversation never grew stagnant. One unpaid trainee, intentional in every gesture, would not tire; he gave clear instruction and crisp dictation to a junior, who copied it in symmetric handwriting. We watched a man chew betel as another swept excrement and stray scraps away to extinguish the smell. A reformed convict explained the microeconomics of his trade: each acquisition, each direct sale, was a gamble against the odds, as unpredictable as a strike in a game of bowling. My daughter-in-law, who studies how leaves release oxygen, read the manual aloud, and no thief would dare rob a place that might, as they say, scrape the very soul.
translation:
也许没有哪个地方像周日集市这样能揭示一个人的性格，那里的环境随着一个个摊位而变化，一则轶事便能概括一生。上一个晴天，在明媚的阳光下，我加入了广场左侧的人群，乐于漫无目的地闲逛，没有固定的议程。一个年轻的小贩，举止懒散却谈吐机灵，正想为一盒各式各样的磁性玩具和一副他希望能让自己发财的紧凑棋具做广告。

附近，一位穿着褪色牛仔裤的爱国者摆出一件形状像行星的装饰物、一套明信片，还有一台旧汽车收音机的外壳。他谈起本地手艺的复兴时充满坚毅，以至于一小群人开始真正产生兴趣。"实际上，"他一边刮去硬币上的泥说，"这些东西比屏幕上任何明星都更有保障。留意过去，它就不会烦扰你。"

这场景几乎是古典的。一位女小提琴手在演奏，墙上最高处挂着一幅公爵的画像，神情既急切又安详。有人开始绣一面旗帜；另一个人慢慢咀嚼一条肉干，旁边或许配着茄子腌菜。整幅全景是一项关于人类多样性的研究，全面而温暖，尽管时不时一个不受欢迎的胳膊肘猛拉会提醒你，在拥挤的柜台前看好自己的座位。

与此同时，一位穿灰外套的公证人在一排排摊位间穿行，宣告一份伪造文件无效，并驳回一项可疑的索赔。他以多年生意磨砺出的眼光解释说，仅仅否认欺诈是不够的，人必须直接反对它。"欠下你还不起的债是欠考虑的，"他对一个紧张的年轻人说，"无视杂乱无章花钱的危险则是鲁莽的。这种习惯能让一个家庭堕落，就像奴隶制曾经碾碎自由人一样确定无疑。"

我买了一幅小版画——一个我未曾预料的发现——还有一个盖子再也合不上的带伤痕的木盒。卖主在收藏家中颇有声望，他乐意再赠送一件，一台厨房用的古董搅拌器，只为帮我个忙。随着白天转凉，高潮时刻到来了：一位脸上有疤痕的老人开始演奏一支萦绕心头的曲子。把美置于价格之上的优待、一贯温和的氛围、讨价还价的乐趣，这一切都让这个集市成为一个我会毫不犹豫地派我的朋友们前往的地方，确信他们离开时同样心满意足。 远足结束前，一名警卫在外围的门口查验我们的身份证件，他的举止是一种不可战胜的纪律的标志。谈话从未变得停滞。一位不拿薪水的受训者，每个动作都饱含用心，毫不疲倦；他向一位新人清晰地讲解、口述，新人则以匀称的字迹抄录。我们看着一个人嚼槟榔，另一个人则把排泄物和零碎残渣扫走以消除气味。一位改过自新的罪犯讲解着他这行的微观经济学：每一次取得、每一次直接的买卖，都是与几率的赌博，像保龄球运动里的全中一样难以预料。我那研究叶片如何释放氧气的儿媳大声念着手册，没有小偷敢去抢劫这样一个据说能刮净灵魂的地方。
